title: Austrostipa dongicola
taxonomy: Austrostipa dongicola is classified within the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the monocot angiosperm group. The genus Austrostipa is further classified as a part of the tribe Danthonieae. This species can be identified by its distinctive characteristics and morphology. It is considered a native Australian plant species.

intro: Austrostipa dongicola, also known as Wonga grass, is a species of perennial grass native to the eastern regions of Australia. Traditionally, Wonga grass has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Wonga grass include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Wonga grass include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
